- A new maintainer can regenerate fixtures using only the doc.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:03.676906157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:36.960388313Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vbs","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:36.960362635Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vmm","title":"E2E: legacy vs Rust parity runner (fixtures + diff logs)","description":"Background:
- We must prove parity by running identical scenarios in legacy and Rust and diffing outputs.

Steps:
- Build a runner that executes scenarios in both runtimes (legacy + Rust).
- Normalize outputs using the harness rules (paths/time/ANSI) before diffing.
- Emit per-scenario diff logs with context, including raw vs normalized output.
- Store diff artifacts for CI review and triage.

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs (extension id, scenario id, runtime).

Acceptance:
- Parity runner completes for the full sample and produces deterministic diffs.
- Failures are actionable with minimal manual digging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:47:11.638706880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:58.553997960Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:36.025190918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:58.553968786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:50.448234186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:54.640846590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-7al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:40.204782716Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xgo","title":"Extensions: extc pipeline (SWC) + cache + compat scanner","description":"Background:
- JS/TS extensions need a deterministic build pipeline to run as-is without Node/Bun.
- The pipeline must rewrite legacy APIs into connector hostcalls and enforce compatibility.

Steps:
- Implement extc build pipeline with SWC: bundle, tree-shake, and emit target artifact (QuickJS bytecode or JS->WASM).
- Run a static compatibility scan (forbidden APIs, Node/Bun globals, unsafe patterns).
- Apply protocol shims/rewrite passes to map legacy APIs to pi connector calls.
- Add artifact cache keyed by sha256(manifest + bundle + engine_version).
- Emit build report metadata (warnings, compatibility flags, required capabilities).

Logging requirements:
- Build logs must include extension id, input hash, and any rejected APIs.

Acceptance:
- A representative JS extension bundles deterministically with cache hits on repeat.
- Compatibility violations are reported with actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:33.029915380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:27.809200249Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:27.809161367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xgo","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:02.648692771Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-yd9","title":"Define extension fixture schema + normalization contract","description":"Background:
- Extension conformance requires a fixture schema that captures inputs and expected outputs precisely.

Steps:
- Define JSON schema for extension fixtures (inputs, outputs, metadata, normalization hints).
- Specify normalization rules (paths/time/ANSI/UUID) and how hints are applied.
- Provide example fixtures for tools, slash commands, and event hooks.
- Document how to add new cases and validate fixtures.

Acceptance:
- Schema can represent tools, slash commands, and event hooks uniformly.
- Fixture validation is deterministic and ready for unit tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:33.255817422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:07:13.327636134Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
